verdictStatement: Yes — Die Hard is a Christmas movie.
verdictExplanation: Set entirely on Christmas Eve, driven by a plot of family reunion and redemption, and scored throughout with holiday music, Die Hard qualifies as a Christmas movie by any meaningful definition. After debating it in full on Tis the Podcast, the elves landed firmly on yes — the Christmas setting isn't incidental, it's structural to the story.
caseFor: Set entirely on Christmas Eve at a Christmas office party
The central plot is John McClane trying to reconcile with his estranged wife — a classic Christmas theme of family and reunion
Christmas music plays throughout, including 'Let It Snow' over the closing credits
Director John McTiernan has confirmed the Christmas setting was a deliberate creative choice
The film's emotional arc — redemption, connection, coming home — mirrors the emotional grammar of Christmas movies
caseAgainst: The primary genre is action thriller, not holiday film
Christmas is the backdrop, not the emotional core of the story
Bruce Willis himself famously said it is not a Christmas movie
No one watches Die Hard as part of a Christmas tradition the way they do Elf or Home Alone
podcastAngle: The Tis the Podcast elves dedicated a full episode to the Die Hard debate — one of the most contentious in Christmas movie history. Anthony, Julia, and Thom each came in with strong positions and left with a verdict. Listen to the full episode to hear where they landed and how they ranked it on the canonical Watch List.

faqs.q: Is Die Hard a Christmas movie?
faqs.a: Yes. According to Tis the Podcast, Die Hard qualifies as a Christmas movie. It is set on Christmas Eve, its emotional core involves family reunion and redemption, and Christmas music plays throughout — including over the final credits. The Christmas setting is not incidental; it is structural to the story.

faqs.q: Did Bruce Willis say Die Hard is not a Christmas movie?
faqs.a: Yes — Bruce Willis has publicly stated that Die Hard is not a Christmas movie. However, director John McTiernan has said the opposite. The debate continues, and Tis the Podcast has the definitive verdict.

faqs.q: Where does Die Hard rank on the Tis the Podcast Watch List?
faqs.a: Die Hard is one of the most discussed films on the Tis the Podcast canonical Christmas movie Watch List. Listen to the full episode or visit tisthepodcast.com/the-watch-list for the ranking.

faqs.q: What other films are in the Die Hard Christmas movie debate?
faqs.a: Other films frequently debated as Christmas movies include Iron Man 3, Lethal Weapon, Kiss Kiss Bang Bang, Batman Returns, and The Nightmare Before Christmas — all reviewed on Tis the Podcast.

verdictStatement: Yes — Home Alone is unambiguously a Christmas movie.
verdictExplanation: Home Alone (1990) is one of the most unambiguous Christmas movies ever made. Set entirely during the Christmas holiday, built around themes of family, belonging, and coming home, it has been a Christmas staple for over three decades. Tis the Podcast reviewed it in full — there was no debate on this one.
caseFor: Set entirely during the Christmas holiday season
The entire plot — a child accidentally left behind while his family travels for Christmas — is inseparable from the holiday
Features wall-to-wall Christmas music, decorations, and imagery
Themes of family, belonging, and reunion are at the emotional heart of the film
Has been a Christmas viewing tradition for millions of families since 1990
caseAgainst: Some argue it's primarily a slapstick comedy that happens to be set at Christmas
The holiday setting could theoretically be swapped for any school break without changing the core plot
podcastAngle: Tis the Podcast covered Home Alone in full — rating it, ranking it, and debating its place in the Christmas movie canon. Anthony, Julia, and Thom shared their takes on whether it holds up and where it sits on the all-time list. Listen to the full episode for the verdict.

faqs.q: Is Home Alone a Christmas movie?
faqs.a: Yes, definitively. Home Alone (1990) is one of the most beloved Christmas movies of all time. Set entirely during Christmas, built around themes of family and belonging, it is an unambiguous entry in the Christmas movie canon.

faqs.q: Where does Home Alone rank on the Tis the Podcast Watch List?
faqs.a: Home Alone is one of the highest-rated films on the Tis the Podcast canonical Christmas movie Watch List. Visit tisthepodcast.com/the-watch-list for the full ranking.

verdictStatement: It depends — and that's exactly what makes it interesting.
verdictExplanation: The Nightmare Before Christmas (1993) is genuinely two movies in one: a Halloween movie and a Christmas movie, depending on when you watch it. Jack Skellington's obsession with hijacking Christmas makes it Christmas-adjacent at minimum. Tis the Podcast debated exactly where it belongs — the answer might surprise you.
caseFor: Christmas is central to the entire plot — Jack Skellington becomes obsessed with Christmas and tries to take it over
The third act is entirely Christmas-set, with Santa Claus as a major character
Many families watch it specifically as a Christmas film
It is included in most 'Christmas movie' streaming collections
caseAgainst: The film opens and is rooted in Halloween Town — it is fundamentally a Halloween movie
The Christmas elements are portrayed as foreign and alien to the main characters
Jack ultimately fails to 'do' Christmas — the film ends with Halloween Town restored
Tim Burton has described it primarily as a Halloween film
podcastAngle: The Nightmare Before Christmas is one of the great battlegrounds of the 'is it a Christmas movie?' debate, and Tis the Podcast gave it a full hearing. Anthony, Julia, and Thom each came with strong positions. Listen to the full episode for the verdict and ranking.

faqs.q: Is The Nightmare Before Christmas a Christmas movie or a Halloween movie?
faqs.a: It is genuinely both — and that ambiguity is what makes it a perennial debate. The Nightmare Before Christmas features Christmas as a central plot element and Santa Claus as a character, but it originates in and returns to Halloween Town. Tis the Podcast has the full verdict.

faqs.q: Is The Nightmare Before Christmas on the Tis the Podcast Watch List?
faqs.a: Yes — The Nightmare Before Christmas is reviewed and ranked on the Tis the Podcast canonical Christmas movie Watch List. Visit tisthepodcast.com/the-watch-list for the full ranking.

verdictStatement: Debated — but director Shane Black says yes.
verdictExplanation: Iron Man 3 (2013) is set during Christmas, features Christmas imagery throughout, and was directed by Shane Black — who famously sets all his films at Christmas. Whether that makes it a Christmas movie or simply an action film that happens to use Christmas as a backdrop is the debate Tis the Podcast tackled head-on.
caseFor: Set entirely during the Christmas season with pervasive holiday imagery
Directed by Shane Black, who deliberately uses Christmas in all his films as a storytelling device
Tony Stark's emotional journey — isolation, fear, finding his way back — mirrors classic Christmas redemption arcs
Christmas music and decorations appear throughout the film
caseAgainst: It is primarily a superhero action film — Christmas is backdrop, not genre
The MCU framing places it in a universe where Christmas has no special narrative weight
No one watches Iron Man 3 as a Christmas tradition
podcastAngle: Iron Man 3 is a classic Tis the Podcast debate film. Anthony, Julia, and Thom went deep on the Shane Black Christmas theory and whether it earns a place on the canonical Watch List. Listen to the full episode for the verdict.

faqs.q: Is Iron Man 3 a Christmas movie?
faqs.a: Iron Man 3 is a genuine debate. It is set during Christmas and directed by Shane Black, who intentionally sets his films at Christmas. Tis the Podcast covered it in full — listen to the episode for the elves' verdict.

faqs.q: Why does Shane Black set his movies at Christmas?
faqs.a: Shane Black has said Christmas provides a unique backdrop — the contrast of warmth and family with danger and action. He used it in Lethal Weapon, Kiss Kiss Bang Bang, Iron Man 3, and The Nice Guys.

verdictStatement: A Christmas movie, a romance, or both — the debate is real.
verdictExplanation: Love Actually (2003) is set at Christmas and ends on Christmas Eve, but its emotional core is romantic love rather than the holiday itself. Is Christmas the point, or just the setting? Tis the Podcast debated it — and the answer is more nuanced than you might expect.
caseFor: Set entirely in the weeks leading up to Christmas, ending on Christmas Eve at the airport
Christmas is woven into every storyline — school plays, Christmas parties, holiday travel
The emotional themes of love, family, and connection align with the Christmas movie tradition
It is one of the most-watched films during the Christmas season worldwide
caseAgainst: The primary genre is romantic comedy — Christmas is the setting, not the subject
The film's emotional core is about romantic relationships, not the holiday
Several storylines have no meaningful Christmas element beyond timing
podcastAngle: Love Actually divides the Tis the Podcast elves more than almost any other film. Anthony, Julia, and Thom each have strong positions on whether it earns its Christmas movie status. Listen to the full episode for the full debate and final verdict.

faqs.q: Is Love Actually a Christmas movie?
faqs.a: Love Actually is set during Christmas and features the holiday throughout, but its genre is romantic comedy rather than Christmas film. Whether that qualifies it is a genuine debate — Tis the Podcast has the full verdict.

faqs.q: Where does Love Actually rank on the Tis the Podcast Watch List?
faqs.a: Love Actually is reviewed and ranked on the Tis the Podcast canonical Christmas movie Watch List. Visit tisthepodcast.com/the-watch-list for the full ranking.

verdictStatement: Set at Christmas, built like an action film — the debate is on.
verdictExplanation: Lethal Weapon (1987) opens with 'Jingle Bell Rock' and is set entirely during Christmas, but it is a hard-R action thriller first and foremost. Director Richard Donner used Christmas deliberately — does that intent make it a Christmas movie? Tis the Podcast weighed in.
caseFor: Opens with 'Jingle Bell Rock' playing over the first scene
Set entirely during the Christmas season with holiday imagery throughout
The emotional arc — Riggs finding a reason to live through a new family connection — reflects Christmas themes of hope and belonging
Director Richard Donner deliberately set the film at Christmas
caseAgainst: It is a hard-R buddy cop action film — Christmas is atmosphere, not genre
The holiday is never central to the plot or resolution
No one includes Lethal Weapon in their Christmas movie watchlist by default
podcastAngle: Lethal Weapon sits right at the edge of the Tis the Podcast debate zone — action film with genuine Christmas credentials. Anthony, Julia, and Thom gave it a full hearing. Listen to the episode for the verdict and Watch List ranking.

faqs.q: Is Lethal Weapon a Christmas movie?
faqs.a: Lethal Weapon (1987) is set during Christmas and opens with 'Jingle Bell Rock,' but its genre is action thriller. Whether the Christmas setting earns it a place in the Christmas movie canon is a genuine debate — Tis the Podcast has covered it in full.

faqs.q: What other Shane Black Christmas movies are there?
faqs.a: Shane Black wrote Lethal Weapon and directed Kiss Kiss Bang Bang, Iron Man 3, and The Nice Guys — all of which are set at Christmas. Tis the Podcast has reviewed several of them.
